sections.paragraph:
ἀνυπέρβλητος, ον (αν-priv., ὑπερβάλλω ‘shoot beyond the mark’; X., Pla. et al.; Polyb. 8, 12, 12; Diod S 1, 55, 10; 13, 56, 5; Dio Chrys. 58 [75], 8; Ael. Aristid. 25, 18K.=43 p. 803 D.; Herm. Wr. 6, 3; 11, 5; SIG 839, 9; 893, 16f; PFlor 382, 48; POxy 1070, 21; 1121, 7; PGM 2, 150; 4, 1201; 1873; 3172; Jdth 16:13; EpArist 92; Philo, Mos. 2, 207; Jos., Bell. 2, 198, Ant. 11, 44) unsurpassable, unexcelled τελειότης perfection 1 Cl 53:5. εὔνοια MPol 17:3. ὡς ἀ. εἶναι τὸ τῆς χαρᾶ μέγεθος so that the full measure of his joy could not be surpassed AcPl Ha 6, 9.—DELG s.v. βάλλω.

priv. priv. = privativum (in grammar: privative, w. negating force)

  1. X. = Xenophon, V–IV b.c.—List 5

Pla Pla , V–IV b.c.; s. also Ps.-Plato—List 5

et al. et al. = et alii (and others)

Polyb Polyb , III–II b.c.—List 5

Diod S Diod S , I b.c.—List 5

Dio Chrys Dio Chrys , I–II a.d.—List 5

Ael. Aristid. Ael. Aristid. = Aelius Aristides, II a.d.—List 5

D D = Didache, except that in a list of manuscripts or as textual variant D refers to Codex Bezae—List 1

Herm. Wr. Herm. Wr. = Hermetic Writings—List 5

SIG SIG = Sylloge Inscriptionum Graecarum3; superscript omitted in text—List 3

PFlor PFlor = Papiri Fiorentini—List 4

POxy POxy = Oxyrhynchus Papyri—List 4

PGM PGM = Papyri Graecae Magicae—List 4

EpArist EpArist = Epistle of Aristeas, II b.c.—List 5

Philo Philo = P. of Alexandria, I b.c.–I a.d.—List 5

Jos. Jos. = Josephus. This abbr. used when follow by title; I a.d.—Lists 5

1 Cl 1 Cl = 1 Clement—List 1

MPol MPol = Martyrdom of Polycarp; after II a.d.—List 1

AcPl Ha AcPl Ha = Acts of Paul, PHamb—List 1

DELG DELG = PChantraine, Dictionnaire étymologique de la langue grecque—List 6

s.v. s.v. = sub voce (under the word, look up the word)

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 91
